title: Context Window Pruning
concept: Managing long conversations requires summarizing past turns, removing old messages, or using sliding-window attention to save tokens.

exercises.prompt: What is the term for the maximum limit of text (tokens) an AI model can process in a single turn?
exercises.explain: The total amount of text an AI can read and write at one time is called its context window (measured in tokens).

cards.title: The AI Amnesia Mystery
cards.body: Have you ever noticed a chatbot suddenly forgetting what you said ten minutes ago? It didn't get bored—it just ran out of memory space! AIs have a strict limit on how many words they can process at once. Let's learn how we prune the chat history to keep them smart and fast.

cards.title: What is Pruning?
cards.body: Context Window Pruning is the process of cleaning up an AI's active memory. Since LLMs can only remember a limited number of words (tokens) at one time, we must summarize past messages, delete very old chat history, or keep a sliding window of the most recent messages to prevent the AI from running out of space.

cards.title: The Busy Waiter
cards.body: Imagine your AI is a waiter taking a giant order at a table. Instead of memorizing every single word spoken since the appetizer, the waiter keeps a tiny notepad. They scribble down the main points ('Table 4 wants steak, no onions') and throw away the polite chit-chat. Pruning does the same by keeping only the core facts so the AI's mind doesn't overflow!

cards.title: Pruning in Action
cards.body: In a coding assistant chatbot, instead of sending 50 previous code drafts back and forth, the system prunes the history. It deletes the early drafts and only sends the latest working code and the last three user questions to the AI, saving thousands of tokens.

cards.title: Does Pruning Make AI Dumb?
cards.body: Many people believe that if we prune chat history, the AI loses all its intelligence. In reality, pruning only removes repetitive, irrelevant, or extremely old messages. By focusing only on what matters right now, the AI actually performs better and responds much faster!

cards.title: Full History vs. Pruned History
cards.body: See how managing the context window changes what the AI actually processes.

left.text: Keeping every single greeting, typo, and old message. The AI gets overwhelmed and runs out of memory.

right.text: Old chat is summarized or trimmed. The AI only gets the active code and the latest question.

exercises.prompt: How does a 'sliding-window' approach manage the AI's context limit?

options.text: It stretches the AI's brain to read infinitely long texts
It only keeps a set number of the most recent messages and discards the oldest ones
It translates all messages into emojis to save space
It forces the user to wait 5 minutes between sending messages

exercises.explain: A sliding window keeps a fixed number of the most recent messages (e.g., only the last 10 messages) and discards the older ones to keep the token count low.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Pruning the context window can help the AI respond faster and save money on API costs.
exercises.explain: Pruning reduces the number of tokens processed, which means the AI has less data to read and can generate a response much quicker.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the strategy for saving space in an AI's memory.
exercises.sentence: To keep the main points of a long chat without using too many tokens, we can summarize the older messages.
exercises.explain: Instead of keeping every single word, we write a short summary of the early conversation to save space while keeping the main context.

exercises.prompt: An app needs to send previous chat history to an AI. Which option shows history that has been pruned using summarization?

options.text: [System]: User said hi. AI said hello. User asked for a joke. AI told a joke about a fish. User said that was funny.
[System]: Send the entire chat history of 4,000 words exactly as it was typed by the user.
[System Summary]: The user asked for help debugging a Python loop. The assistant provided a fix. (Current Turn): User: 'It works, but how do I speed it up?'

exercises.explain: Option C is the AI generated chat history because it shows a summarized recap of the past context, which is a key technique of context pruning. Options A and B just show raw, unpruned messages.

exercises.prompt: Why is context window pruning absolutely necessary for long, ongoing chat sessions with an AI?

options.text: It makes the AI write much longer answers than before
It keeps the conversation within token limits so the AI doesn't forget the current topic
It permanently deletes the AI's core training weights
It allows the AI to run without using any electricity at all

exercises.explain: Pruning is essential because AI models have a strict limit on how much text they can read at once. If we don't clean out old or unimportant tokens, the AI will completely forget the current conversation or refuse to answer!
